answer:
**The Falsafa corpus contains a complete translation of Mirza Ghalib's *Diwan-e-Ghalib* (239 ghazals), but searches across its English translations for phrases like "heart" with "meat," "piece of meat," "flesh," "morsel," "beak," "eye," "glance," "liver," "core," and close variations yielded no matching couplet.**[^1]

The closest passage—perhaps a loose paraphrase in memory—is the opening sher of ghazal 35 (*Dil se teri nigaah jigar tak utar gayi*), where a lover's "glance" pierces "from my heart into my core" (*jigar* / liver, a poetic stand-in for deep passion):  
> Your glance passed from my heart into my core;  
> With a single charm, their will to fight is gone.[^2]

No reference to the heart as "a piece of meat" or an "eyes' beak" appears in the English bodies (or wiki summaries). If this is from a different edition, poet, or non-corpus source, it's not covered here.[^3]

[^1]: Multiple `search_corpus` queries (e.g., "heart meat beak eye"; "\"heart\" \"piece of meat\" eye beak"; "\"beak\""; "heart \"piece\" OR \"flesh\" OR \"meat\" OR \"morsel\""; "\"heart\" \"glance\""; "\"heart\" OR \"liver\" OR \"core\" \"eye\" OR \"glance\" OR \"gaze\""), all on *Diwan-E-Ghalib* via author filter.

[^2]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 35 (*Dil se teri nigaah jigar tak utar gayi*), [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/35-dil-se-teri-nigaah-jigar-tak-utar-gayi/translation/#p-4ab0d4).

[^3]: Confirmed via `read_wiki` on work and candidates (e.g., ch. 35, 162); `list_works` (author: ghalib); `list_chapters`; no hits in `read_chapter` (ch. 35 full text). Corpus has 37 works total; Ghalib's is the only Urdu poetry match.
